data.description:
Add `cmux snapshot` / `cmux restore` / `cmux list-snapshots` subcommands that capture a workspace's layout + per-surface state to JSON and rebuild it later — with bit-exact Claude session resume via `cc --resume <session-id>`.

**Why:** `claude --resume <id>` rehydrates full context and history, but nobody uses it because nobody tracks the IDs. Pair it with a layout snapshot and "come back to this tomorrow" becomes `cmux restore morning-work`.

**Plan:** docs/c11mux-snapshot-restore-plan.md

**Verified preconditions:**
- `cc --resume <id>` works (cc is an alias; flags pass through).
- SessionStart hook receives `session_id` on stdin JSON.
- `terminal_type = claude-code` is already being written to surface manifests today.

**Key design choices:**
- Agent-agnostic snapshot: reads `cmux tree --all --json` + per-surface manifests. No private files, no title heuristics.
- Claude session ID captured by an **operator-installed** SessionStart hook that writes `claude.session_id` + `cwd` to the surface manifest. c11mux does not install the hook (principle). Hook is documented in the `cmux` skill.
- `cmux tree --json` is flat — each pane carries `split_path` breadcrumbs. Restore reconstructs the binary split tree from the paths.
- Known-type restart registry inside `cmux restore`: `claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`. Extensible per-agent without schema changes.
- Prior art: sanghun0724/cmux-claude-skills. Not adopting directly — its implementation uses c11mux's private session JSON, spinner-char detection, and fuzzy session-ID matching. We use the manifest instead.

**Phases:**
1. Subcommands + terminal surfaces + single workspace + Claude resume.
2. Browser/markdown surfaces + `--all` flag.
3. Skill docs + SessionStart hook snippet.
4. codex/kimi/opencode registry rows.

**Principle check:** fits "unopinionated about the terminal" — operates on c11mux primitives, writes only to `~/.cmux-snapshots/`, does not touch tenant settings files.

**Related:** supersedes CMUX-4 (manual Claude session index) via the hook-driven approach. Restore preserves CMUX-11 pane manifests + CMUX-14 lineage chains verbatim.
data.title: c11mux snapshot & restore: bit-exact Claude session resume + layout rebuild

data.from: c11mux snapshot & restore: bit-exact Claude session resume + layout rebuild
data.to: c11mux workspace persistence: Blueprints (declarative) + Snapshots (live state) + session resume

provenance.reason: Fold Blueprint concept (Markdown workspace definitions) into ticket per discussion; note supersession of CMUX-4 and CMUX-5

data.to:
Add `cmux workspace new`, `cmux snapshot`, `cmux restore`, and `cmux list-snapshots` subcommands that support two distinct persistence concepts — **Blueprints** (declarative Markdown definitions of initial workspace shape) and **Snapshots** (exact serialized live state) — unified under a single workspace-persistence story with bit-exact Claude session resume.

**Why:** `claude --resume <id>` rehydrates full context but nobody tracks the IDs. Pair it with declarative Blueprints (for starting fresh work) and auto-captured Snapshots (for "come back to this tomorrow") and the terminal becomes a persistent workspace, not a disposable shell.

**Plan:** `docs/c11mux-snapshot-restore-plan.md` (update to reflect Blueprint addition).

## Two concepts, one system

| | Blueprint | Snapshot |
|---|---|---|
| Purpose | Define initial workspace shape | Preserve exact live state |
| Format | Markdown (Obsidian-compatible) | JSON |
| Author | Human or agent (hand-written or via `cmux workspace export-blueprint`) | Auto-generated by c11mux |
| Storage | Per-repo (`.cmux/blueprints/*.md`) + per-user (`~/.config/cmux/blueprints/*.md`) | Per-user (`~/.cmux-snapshots/`) |
| Lifecycle | Checked into git, shareable, version-controlled | Opaque, personal, crash-recovery oriented |
| Applied | At workspace creation only | On restart / manual restore |
| Idempotency | Creates the workspace as defined; no merge-into-live semantics | Rehydrates exact tree from JSON |

Both reuse the **known-type restart registry** (`claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`) so a Blueprint can embed a `claude.session_id` field and resume the same context a Snapshot would.

## Blueprint format (sketch)

Markdown with YAML frontmatter and structured sections. Exact schema TBD during Phase 2. Example shape:

```markdown
---
name: debug-auth
description: Auth module debugging layout
---

## Panes
- main  | `cc`              | cwd: ~/repo          | claude.session_id: abc123
- logs  | `tail -f log.txt` | split: right of main
- tests | `vitest --watch`  | split: below logs
```

**Idempotency rule:** Blueprints apply only at workspace creation. Once alive, the workspace is the user's to mutate. "Re-apply" = close and reopen.

## New-workspace picker

Empty state of `cmux workspace new` (with no args) shows a merged, recency-sorted picker:

1. Per-repo Blueprints (in-tree, shareable)
2. Per-user Blueprints (personal muscle memory)
3. Built-in starter Blueprints (ship with c11mux)

Selecting one materializes the workspace. This subsumes the recovery-banner UX from (superseded) CMUX-5.

## Verified preconditions

- `cc --resume <id>` works (cc is an alias; flags pass through).
- SessionStart hook receives `session_id` on stdin JSON.
- `terminal_type = claude-code` is already written to surface manifests today.

## Key design choices

- **Agent-agnostic.** Snapshots read `cmux tree --all --json` + per-surface manifests. No private files, no title heuristics.
- **Observe-from-outside session capture.** Claude session ID written by an **operator-installed** SessionStart hook. c11mux never installs the hook (principle). Hook snippet documented in the `cmux` skill.
- **Flat tree with breadcrumbs.** `cmux tree --json` is flat; each pane carries `split_path`. Restore reconstructs the binary split tree from paths.
- **Known-type restart registry.** `claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`. Extensible per agent type without schema changes; Blueprints and Snapshots share this registry.
- **Markdown for Blueprints, JSON for Snapshots.** Blueprints are authored/reviewed by humans and agents; Snapshots are machine output. Different audiences, different formats.
- **No merge-into-live semantics.** Blueprints only apply at creation; Snapshots only apply at restore. No ambiguous diff-and-sync behavior.

## Phases

1. **Core subcommands + Snapshots.** `cmux snapshot`, `cmux restore`, `cmux list-snapshots`. Terminal surfaces, single workspace, Claude resume via restart registry.
2. **Blueprint format + picker.** Define Blueprint Markdown schema and parser. `cmux workspace new --blueprint <path>`. New-workspace picker shows recent Blueprints. `cmux workspace export-blueprint` captures live layout as a Blueprint for an agent or human to commit.
3. **Browser/markdown surfaces + `--all` flag.** Extend both Blueprints and Snapshots to non-terminal surfaces.
4. **Skill docs + SessionStart hook snippet.** Document Blueprint authoring conventions in the `cmux` skill. Document the operator-installed hook.
5. **codex/kimi/opencode registry rows.** Add per-agent restart behavior.

## Out of scope (deferred to own tickets)

- **c11mux-manager agent personality** — a dedicated agent whose job is authoring and evolving Blueprints, plus a skill file other agents use to read/write them. Separate ticket once Blueprint format stabilizes.
- **Lattice↔Blueprint frontmatter seam** — ticket frontmatter field (`workspace:`) that auto-materializes the Blueprint on "start work." Minimal version (Markdown link in the ticket body → operator clicks to open) works without any integration. Frontmatter seam deferred until we feel the pain of not having it.

## Principle check

- "Unopinionated about the terminal": operates on c11mux primitives, writes only to `~/.cmux-snapshots/` (Snapshots) and `.cmux/blueprints/` or `~/.config/cmux/blueprints/` (Blueprints). Does not touch tenant tool settings.
- Observe-from-outside: Claude session capture via operator-installed hook, never c11mux-installed.

## Supersedes

- **CMUX-4** (manual Claude session index) — hook-driven capture in this ticket replaces JSONL discovery/disambiguation logic.
- **CMUX-5** (recovery UI banner) — subsumed by the new-workspace picker + restart registry.

Restore preserves CMUX-11 pane manifests + CMUX-14 lineage chains verbatim.

## Prior art

sanghun0724/cmux-claude-skills — not adopting directly; its implementation uses c11mux's private session JSON, spinner-char detection, and fuzzy session-ID matching. We use the public manifest instead.

drolosoft/cmux-resurrect (crex) — community tool for upstream manaflow-ai/cmux with an adjacent design (save/restore + Markdown "Blueprints" + watch daemon + template gallery). Inspired the Blueprint/Snapshot split named here. We deliberately keep the c11mux primitive narrower: snapshot/restore + Blueprint format as core primitives; templates, REPL, and daemon remain ecosystem territory.

New-workspace picker + restart registry subsume the recovery banner UX

data.to:
Add `cmux workspace new`, `cmux snapshot`, `cmux restore`, and `cmux list-snapshots` subcommands that support two distinct persistence concepts — **Blueprints** (declarative Markdown definitions of initial workspace shape) and **Snapshots** (exact serialized live state) — unified under a single workspace-persistence story with bit-exact Claude session resume.

**Why:** `claude --resume <id>` rehydrates full context but nobody tracks the IDs. Pair it with declarative Blueprints (for starting fresh work) and auto-captured Snapshots (for "come back to this tomorrow") and the terminal becomes a persistent workspace, not a disposable shell.

## Plan freshness warning

The checked-in plan is currently out of date.

- Lattice plan file: `.lattice/plans/task_01KPMTEY4WGECM9MNZ4XARN7Y6.md`
- Referenced docs plan: `docs/c11mux-snapshot-restore-plan.md`

As of 2026-04-21, the Lattice task body is the more current source of truth. The plan file still describes the older snapshot/restore-only shape and does not yet include Blueprints, `workspace new --blueprint`, or the app-side workspace-apply transaction described below. The docs plan path referenced by the ticket was not present when checked. Before implementation, revise or replace the plan so it matches this task body.

## Dogfood finding: blueprint materialization must be app-side, not shell choreography

A 2026-04-21 dogfood attempt to create five custom c11 workspaces through shell automation exposed a failure mode this ticket should explicitly eliminate. The slow path was not layout creation itself; it was composing a workspace by issuing many independent CLI/socket commands in sequence:

- `new-workspace`
- `new-pane` / `new-surface`
- `set-title`
- `set-description`
- repeated `set-metadata`
- `notify`
- follow-up `tree` calls to rediscover refs

That approach creates many process launches, many socket round trips, and many race windows while AppKit/SwiftUI is still attaching panes and surfaces. One transient stall in a legacy/socket command can make the entire batch appear hung.

**Design requirement:** Blueprints and snapshot restore must compile to an app-side workspace apply transaction. They must not be implemented as an internal loop that shells out to existing CLI commands or simulates a human/agent running split commands one by one.

## Core primitive: WorkspaceApplyPlan / WorkspaceLayout

Introduce a declarative in-app plan value that all workspace materialization flows use.

Suggested shape:

```swift
struct WorkspaceApplyPlan: Codable {
    var workspace: WorkspaceSpec
    var panes: [PaneSpec]
    var layout: LayoutTreeSpec
    var surfaces: [SurfaceSpec]
}

struct SurfaceSpec: Codable {
    var type: SurfaceType          // terminal | browser | markdown
    var title: String?
    var description: String?
    var cwd: String?
    var command: String?
    var url: String?
    var file: String?
    var metadata: [String: JSONValue]
}
```

The executor should run inside c11mux/c11 as one logical operation:

1. Create the workspace.
2. Build the bonsplit/layout tree.
3. Create terminal/browser/markdown surfaces.
4. Apply title, description, pane metadata, surface metadata, and workspace metadata during creation.
5. Return all created refs plus per-step timing and structured errors.

This is the same architectural direction surfaced in the CMUX-15 review pack: `WorkspaceLayout` as a value plus an app-side executor, rather than one-off procedural split code. Welcome quad, default grid, Blueprints, Snapshots, demo fixtures, and future Lattice-driven layouts should converge on this primitive.

## Two concepts, one system

| | Blueprint | Snapshot |
|---|---|---|
| Purpose | Define initial workspace shape | Preserve exact live state |
| Format | Markdown (Obsidian-compatible), compiling to `WorkspaceApplyPlan` | JSON, restoring through `WorkspaceApplyPlan` or a close equivalent |
| Author | Human or agent (hand-written or via `cmux workspace export-blueprint`) | Auto-generated by c11mux |
| Storage | Per-repo (`.cmux/blueprints/*.md`) + per-user (`~/.config/cmux/blueprints/*.md`) | Per-user (`~/.cmux-snapshots/`) |
| Lifecycle | Checked into git, shareable, version-controlled | Opaque, personal, crash-recovery oriented |
| Applied | At workspace creation only | On restart / manual restore |
| Idempotency | Creates the workspace as defined; no merge-into-live semantics | Rehydrates exact tree from JSON |

Both reuse the **known-type restart registry** (`claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`) so a Blueprint can embed a `claude.session_id` field and resume the same context a Snapshot would.

## New socket / CLI surface

Add a v2 socket method for programmatic use:

```text
workspace.apply(plan, options) -> { workspace_ref, pane_refs, surface_refs, timings, warnings }
```

CLI wrappers:

```bash
cmux workspace new --blueprint <path>
cmux workspace export-blueprint --workspace <ref> --out <path>
cmux workspace apply --file <path-or-json>       # optional lower-level/debug surface
cmux snapshot [--workspace <ref>|--all]
cmux restore <snapshot-id-or-path>
cmux list-snapshots
```

The CLI should send one structured request to the app. The app should perform the creation, lifecycle waiting, metadata application, and ref assignment internally.

## Blueprint format (sketch)

Markdown with YAML frontmatter and structured sections. Exact schema TBD during Phase 2. Example shape:

```markdown
---
name: debug-auth
description: Auth module debugging layout
---

## Panes
- main  | `cc`              | cwd: ~/repo          | claude.session_id: abc123
- logs  | `tail -f log.txt` | split: right of main
- tests | `vitest --watch`  | split: below logs
```

**Idempotency rule:** Blueprints apply only at workspace creation. Once alive, the workspace is the user's to mutate. "Re-apply" = close and reopen.

## New-workspace picker

Empty state of `cmux workspace new` (with no args) shows a merged, recency-sorted picker:

1. Per-repo Blueprints (in-tree, shareable)
2. Per-user Blueprints (personal muscle memory)
3. Built-in starter Blueprints (ship with c11mux)

Selecting one materializes the workspace through the same `WorkspaceApplyPlan` executor. This subsumes the recovery-banner UX from (superseded) CMUX-5.

## Verified preconditions

- `cc --resume <id>` works (cc is an alias; flags pass through).
- SessionStart hook receives `session_id` on stdin JSON.
- `terminal_type = claude-code` is already written to surface manifests today.

## Key design choices

- **Agent-agnostic.** Snapshots read `cmux tree --all --json` + per-surface manifests. No private files, no title heuristics.
- **Observe-from-outside session capture.** Claude session ID written by an **operator-installed** SessionStart hook. c11mux never installs the hook (principle). Hook snippet documented in the `cmux` skill.
- **Flat tree with breadcrumbs.** `cmux tree --json` is flat; each pane carries `split_path`. Restore reconstructs the binary split tree from paths.
- **Known-type restart registry.** `claude-code` + session_id → `cc --resume <id>`. Extensible per agent type without schema changes; Blueprints and Snapshots share this registry.
- **Markdown for Blueprints, JSON for Snapshots.** Blueprints are authored/reviewed by humans and agents; Snapshots are machine output. Different audiences, different formats.
- **No merge-into-live semantics.** Blueprints only apply at creation; Snapshots only apply at restore. No ambiguous diff-and-sync behavior.
- **Creation-time metadata.** Title, description, role, status, progress, pane metadata, surface metadata, and workspace metadata are applied as part of the app-side operation, not as post-creation CLI calls.
- **Readiness is explicit.** The executor should distinguish `created`, `attached`, `rendered`, and `ready` states where practical. Callers can choose whether to return after creation or wait for readiness with a bounded timeout.
- **Structured failure.** Partial failures return which step failed and which refs were created. Avoid silent hangs.

## Phases

0. **WorkspaceApplyPlan / WorkspaceLayout executor.** Extract or introduce the app-side declarative layout/materialization primitive. Re-express existing welcome/default-grid machinery through it where practical, or at least design the primitive so they can converge.
1. **Core subcommands + Snapshots.** `cmux snapshot`, `cmux restore`, `cmux list-snapshots`. Terminal surfaces, single workspace, Claude resume via restart registry.
2. **Blueprint format + picker.** Define Blueprint Markdown schema and parser. `cmux workspace new --blueprint <path>`. New-workspace picker shows recent Blueprints. `cmux workspace export-blueprint` captures live layout as a Blueprint for an agent or human to commit.
3. **Browser/markdown surfaces + `--all` flag.** Extend both Blueprints and Snapshots to non-terminal surfaces.
4. **Skill docs + SessionStart hook snippet.** Document Blueprint authoring conventions in the `cmux` skill. Document the operator-installed hook.
5. **codex/kimi/opencode registry rows.** Add per-agent restart behavior.

## Performance / reliability acceptance

- A single `workspace.apply` call can create a five-workspace mixed fixture, or five individual blueprint calls can do so, without unbounded waits.
- A mixed workspace containing terminal, browser, markdown, titles, descriptions, and metadata is materialized through one app-side operation rather than N shell/socket calls.
- The executor returns per-step timings in debug/JSON output.
- Any wait for surface readiness is bounded and named in the error.
- Regression test fixture: create at least five poetic/custom workspaces with mixed terminal/browser/markdown surfaces and metadata. Target expectation: completes in about two seconds on a normal dev machine, or fails fast with a named timeout.

## Out of scope (deferred to own tickets)

- **General socket reliability hardening.** CLI response deadlines, tracing, v1 notification migration, and replacing risky `DispatchQueue.main.sync` socket handlers are important but belong to a separate automation reliability ticket. CMUX-37 should rely on that platform work, not absorb all of it.
- **c11mux-manager agent personality** — a dedicated agent whose job is authoring and evolving Blueprints, plus a skill file other agents use to read/write them. Separate ticket once Blueprint format stabilizes.
- **Lattice↔Blueprint frontmatter seam** — ticket frontmatter field (`workspace:`) that auto-materializes the Blueprint on "start work." Minimal version (Markdown link in the ticket body → operator clicks to open) works without any integration. Frontmatter seam deferred until we feel the pain of not having it.

## Principle check

- "Unopinionated about the terminal": operates on c11mux primitives, writes only to `~/.cmux-snapshots/` (Snapshots) and `.cmux/blueprints/` or `~/.config/cmux/blueprints/` (Blueprints). Does not touch tenant tool settings.
- Observe-from-outside: Claude session capture via operator-installed hook, never c11mux-installed.
- Automation is a first-class consumer: workspace materialization must be structured, bounded, inspectable, and fast.

## Supersedes

- **CMUX-4** (manual Claude session index) — hook-driven capture in this ticket replaces JSONL discovery/disambiguation logic.
- **CMUX-5** (recovery UI banner) — subsumed by the new-workspace picker + restart registry.

Restore preserves CMUX-11 pane manifests + CMUX-14 lineage chains verbatim.

## Prior art

sanghun0724/cmux-claude-skills — not adopting directly; its implementation uses c11mux's private session JSON, spinner-char detection, and fuzzy session-ID matching. We use the public manifest instead.

drolosoft/cmux-resurrect (crex) — community tool for upstream manaflow-ai/cmux with an adjacent design (save/restore + Markdown "Blueprints" + watch daemon + template gallery). Inspired the Blueprint/Snapshot split named here. We deliberately keep the c11mux primitive narrower: snapshot/restore + Blueprint format as core primitives; templates, REPL, and daemon remain ecosystem territory.

Goal: close CMUX-37 by landing markdown blueprints + snapshot manifest layer + restore noise + workspace --help + CLI socket safety in one cohesive PR off cmux-37/final-push.

Plan written by agent:claude-opus-4-7-cmux-37-plan to .lattice/plans/task_01KPMTEY4WGECM9MNZ4XARN7Y6.md.

Summary:
- Scope: markdown blueprint format (.md + YAML frontmatter, .config/c11/ rename with JSON back-compat), snapshot-set manifest layer (--all writes pointer file + polymorphic restore), restore-diagnostic reclassification (capture-side + apply-side fix for the 7 spurious failures), workspace --help routing fix, C11_SOCKET env var precedence + auto-discovery stderr breadcrumb.
- Recommended commit grouping: 8 commits — workspace help routing → C11_SOCKET + log → executor diagnostics → capture title strip → markdown parser/writer → blueprint dir rename + .md default → snapshot manifest + restore polymorphism → ticket title/description housekeeping.
- Parallelization: two parallel impl siblings — A handles workstreams 1+2 (markdown + manifest, the bulk; mostly new files), B handles 3+4+5 (small surgical edits on diagnostics, help dispatch, socket resolution). Disjoint regions in CLI/c11.swift; no merge conflicts expected. Recommend B lands first since the C11_SOCKET fix prevents wrong-socket footguns during subsequent validation.
- Localization: yes, ~5 new String(localized:) keys (markdown parse error messages + auto-discovery line). Translator phase fans out per-locale across ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru. Help text bodies stay plain English to match the existing subcommandUsage precedent.
- Out-of-scope (DO NOT ship in this PR): CMUX_SOCKET_PATH deprecation warning, codex/opencode/kimi restart-registry rows, mailbox topic fan-out, JSON blueprint reader removal, typing-latency hot-path edits, persistent writes to tenant config, public cmux CLI alias rename, submodule edits.

Plan ready for delegator review and Impl phase launch.

Plan validated; status bumped planned → in_progress. Plan recommends two impl siblings serialized in worktree:
- Sibling B (next, lands commits 1-4): workstream 4 (workspace --help routing), workstream 5 (C11_SOCKET safety + auto-discovery breadcrumb), workstream 3 (executor diagnostic reclassification + capture-side title strip).
- Sibling A (follows B, lands commits 5-7): workstream 1 (markdown blueprint format) + workstream 2 (snapshot manifest layer).
- Commit 8 (housekeeping: rename ticket title c11mux→c11, strip stale plan-freshness warning) handled by delegator post-impl, pre-PR.
B-first order makes subsequent validation safer (C11_SOCKET fix lands early; smoke build can use the env var instead of explicit --socket). Spawning B now.

Spun out C11-24 ('codex + opencode session resume in c11 restart registry') for the deferred Phase 5 work — the registry primitive lands in this PR via the cc row; codex/opencode are additive rows + a skill update, no schema change. Keeps the final-push PR cohesive.

Shipped via PR #118 ("CMUX-37 final push: close five 2026-05-03 smoke-test gaps"), merged 2026-05-04T23:37:11Z as commit cbae93d3 on main. All five workstreams from the final-push plan landed: workspace --help routing, C11_SOCKET env precedence, restore-diagnostic reclassification (apply-side severity + capture-side title strip), markdown blueprint format with .config/c11/ rename and JSON back-compat, and snapshot manifest layer with polymorphic restore. Earlier phases shipped via PRs #32, #75, #77, #78, #79. The 19:12Z plan-agent comment about a C11-24 codex/opencode-resume spinout is moot — Phase 5 codex/opencode/kimi registry rows already shipped in PR #79 commit 4e4ca5a8. No follow-up ticket needed. Closing.
